text.title: Experiment Ledger Discipline Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules anchoring the experiment ledger. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Ledger entries MUST be immutable. Any edit creates a new versioned row with `delta_reason` and `prev_entry_id`.

rationale.source: Append-only data store discipline (Pat Helland, 'Immutability Changes Everything', 2015)

rule.rationale: In-place edits destroy historical context that the ledger exists to preserve.

rule.statement: Every entry MUST name a producer (handle), a consumer (handle or named agent), and `decision_fed` (which downstream decision the entry feeds). Entries without these fields are rejected as vanity logs.

rationale.source: Anti-vanity-metric practice (Eric Ries, The Lean Startup, 2011)

rule.rationale: Without a consumer + decision, entries pile up unread.

rule.statement: The canonical ledger search (filter by tag + status + cycle) MUST return within 5 seconds on the storage layer chosen. If query latency drifts above 5s, fix indexing before adding more entries.
rule.rationale: Useless slow data is worse than no data; if humans can't query it within a meeting, they revert to memory.

rule.statement: Closing an entry (status=closed) MUST include a `learning_summary` ≥ 80 chars + at least one `tag` from the frozen taxonomy. Closing without learning is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a learning summary, the entry has data but no insight; future-self cannot extract value.

rule.statement: When new results invalidate a prior entry, the prior entry's `superseded_by` field MUST point to the new entry's id. Direct mutation of the prior entry is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Invalidating without superseded_by chains loses the path from old learning to new.
